hypothesis: Augmenting universal MLIP training sets with a small (≤2% of total samples) curated set of DFT-calculated out-of-equilibrium MOF metal–linker coordination environments will increase the fraction of MOFs that complete 50 ps NpT simulations without >10% volume change and reduce mean absolute errors in predicted bulk modulus and heat capacity on MOFSimBench by at least 25% relative to the same uMLIP trained without these MOF-specific samples.
